page: Microsoft Azure E20a_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EAv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and is equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is provisioned with 160.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 512 GB of bundled local storage.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng falls into the bottom 10%, representing a notable bottleneck for single-threaded tasks. However, multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores align with the middle 50% average. Memory subsystem performance is a key strength, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25% (strong), supported by an average uncached read memory bandwidth of 78.56 GB/sec and write bandwidth of 44.51 GB/sec.

This server is configured for memory-intensive workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. It achieves average performance in database operations (6,826.49 operations/sec in PassMark) and Redis SET operations (20.38 million operations/sec). While it handles small-model LLM inference at average speeds, text generation on medium 7B models is weak (6.82 tokens/sec). The qualitative cost efficiency of the E20a_v4 is driven by its high memory density relative to its 20 vCPUs, making it suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data-processing tasks where memory capacity is prioritized over raw single-core compute speed.
description: Microsoft Azure E20a_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EAv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs (10 physical cores) running at 3.525 GHz, 160.0 GB of RAM, and 512 GB of bundled local storage. While stress-ng single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10%, the server demonstrates average multi-core and software compilation capabilities. Its primary strength lies in its memory subsystem, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and uncached read bandwidth of 78.56 GB/sec. Lacking GPU acceleration, this instance is designed for memory-heavy workloads. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data-processing tasks that benefit from its high 8.0 GB per core memory allocation, though it exhibits weaker performance in single-threaded operations and medium-model LLM text generation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20a_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM featuring 20 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 512 GB local storage, delivering strong memory performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20a_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 512 GB local storage, built for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high memory density, and bundled local storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 20 dedicated vCPUs and 10 physical cores.
Equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, offering a high 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio.
Includes 512 GB of bundled local storage for data caching and temporary files.
Strong memory subsystem performance, ranking in the top 25% of PassMark benchmarks.
Best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and memory-intensive data processing.
Tradeoff includes poor single-core CPU performance, ranking in the bottom 10%.
